mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS held stable at the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate and pushes investors into risk assets in search of yield, producing a durable positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as long as rates remain pinned near zero.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during ZIRP periods are not significantly higher than non-ZIRP baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A confirmed decline in UNRATE of at least 0.2 percentage points over two consecutive monthly readings signals labor market repair that boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by >= 0.2pp over 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: In periods when the UNRATE decline trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive less than 55% of the time, or the trigger produces no statistically significant excess return versus unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned at or below 0.25% and PAYEMS 3-month average change simultaneously turns positive, the joint signal of accommodative policy plus labor market inflection marks a credible recovery turning point that markets have not fully priced, producing above-baseline positive returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change turns positive while FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,642 thousand (revised) as of 2010-05-01 with FEDFUNDS at 0.2%
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with labor market inflection
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show 20-day forward return hit rate indistinguishable from single-factor FEDFUNDS-only or PAYEMS-only triggers (interaction adds no incremental rank IC > 0.02), rejecting the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-financial-crisis ZIRP regime, a rebound in HOUST such that its 3-month moving average rises above the prior 6-month average signals renewed housing-sector activity that feeds through construction employment and household wealth effects into broader risk asset demand, lifting S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises above prior 6-month average while FEDFUNDS <= 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised to 687 thousand units as of 2010-04-01 vintage
regime_hint: post-financial-crisis housing recovery regime under ZIRP
failure_condition: HOUST moving-average crossover signal fails to precede positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of triggered instances during ZIRP windows, or effect disappears once FEDFUNDS conditioning is removed.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output momentum that is rapidly incorporated into near-term equity pricing, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns as production data confirm the recovery trajectory.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 90.5991 vs vintage 102.2666 as of 2010-04-01, indicating strong upward industrial trend
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.02 across any rolling 1-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month RSAFS growth rate exceeding 0.5% signals a surge in consumer spending strength that historically precedes upward revisions to corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month in a consumer-led recovery regime.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised to 346,858 million vs vintage 362,517 million as of 2010-05-01
failure_condition: Triggered RSAFS growth episodes produce 20-day forward SPX positive-return hit rate not significantly above the unconditional 65.5% base rate (p > 0.05), or hit rate falls below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation falls below 1% while FEDFUNDS remains at or below 0.25%, the combination signals a deflationary liquidity trap in which monetary policy has lost traction, raising real debt burdens and corporate margin risk, which suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.403 (revised) as of 2010-04-01 with low inflation trend and FEDFUNDS at 0.2%
regime_hint: deflationary risk regime near zero lower bound
failure_condition: During joint low-inflation/zero-rate trigger windows, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, contradicting the deflation-trap suppression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Following an NBER recession trough, a GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeding 3% marks confirmation of a durable economic recovery that has not yet been fully reflected in equity valuations, leading to above-baseline positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth exceeds 3% following an NBER recession trough
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised value 16582.71 vs vintage 13248.2 as of 2010-01-01, indicating post-recession rebound
failure_condition: Triggered GDPC1 growth episodes show 60-day forward SPX positive-return hit rate statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or GDPC1 revision risk causes signal reversal in over 30% of vintages.
